name: A-160-2
description: Clock/trigger divider with 7 outputs and three switchable divisor sets: powers of two, primes, or integers. Two output modes — Gate (50% binary) and Trigger (AND'd with clock, inherits its pulsewidth). Jumper-selectable clock edge, reset polarity, output polarity.

controls.name: Division Mode
controls.description: 3-position switch picks which divisor set the seven outputs use.

controls.name: Output Mode
controls.description: Selects between standard divided gates and trigger-masked pulses.
controls.detail: Gate: 50% duty binary divide · Trigger: output AND clock, pulsewidth = clock pulsewidth

controls.name: Clock Edge (jumper)
controls.description: Internal jumper selects rising or falling edge of clock input as the count trigger.

controls.name: Gate Output Polarity (jumper)
controls.description: Inverts all seven outputs when removed.

controls.name: Reset Input Polarity (jumper)
controls.description: Polarity for reset: level mode picks high/low; edge mode picks rising/falling.

inputs.name: Clock In
inputs.description: Digital clock/trigger input. Accepts any pulse source — LFO square, MIDI-sync gate, sequencer trigger.

inputs.name: Reset In
inputs.description: Synchronously resets all divider counters. Level or edge mode and polarity are jumper-selected.

outputs.name: Out 1 (÷2)
outputs.description: Divided clock — ÷2 in all three modes.

behaviors.name: Binary bar/beat divisions
behaviors.description: Standard powers-of-two divisions produce musical bar, half, quarter, eighth relationships when clock is a 16th or 32nd. Ideal for song-structure gates.

behaviors.name: Prime-number polyrhythms
behaviors.description: Divisors share no common factors, so outputs drift apart maximally. ÷11 and ÷13 only re-align every 143 clocks, yielding long, non-repeating rhythmic textures.

behaviors.name: Odd-meter integer grid
behaviors.description: Sequential integer divides give triplet, quintuplet, septuplet relationships useful for odd-meter patterns that still resolve periodically.

behaviors.name: Trigger mode pulsewidth masking
behaviors.description: Outputs are AND'd with the raw clock, so each divided pulse takes the clock's pulsewidth rather than a 50% gate. Useful for drum-style triggers that sit inside the clock phase instead of stretching across it.

behaviors.name: Synchronous reset on bar boundary
behaviors.description: A reset pulse per bar realigns every output to phase 0, preventing drift when the divider is re-entered mid-song or when the clock is stopped/started.

behaviors.name: Hardware-configured polarity
behaviors.description: Clock edge, output polarity, reset level vs edge, and reset polarity are all jumper-selectable on the PCB — no front-panel way to change. Relevant when interfacing non-Doepfer gate standards.

patchIdeas.name: Classic rhythm scaffolding
patchIdeas.patch: Master clock (16ths) → Clock In · POW2 mode · Out 1 = 8ths · Out 2 = quarters · Out 3 = half · use for kick/snare/hat
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-160-2:
* Division Mode: POW2 · Output Mode: Gate
- Master Clock (Clk) c> A-160-2 (Clock In)
- A-160-2 (Out 1 (÷2)) c> Hat (Trig)
- A-160-2 (Out 2) c> Snare (Trig)
- A-160-2 (Out 3) c> Kick (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Prime polyrhythm drone
patchIdeas.patch: PRIME mode · Out 4 (÷7), Out 5 (÷11), Out 6 (÷13) each trigger slow envelopes on separate drones
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-160-2:
* Division Mode: PRIME · Output Mode: Gate
- Master Clock (Clk) c> A-160-2 (Clock In)
- A-160-2 (Out 4) g> ADSR 1 (Gate)
- A-160-2 (Out 5) g> ADSR 2 (Gate)
- A-160-2 (Out 6) g> ADSR 3 (Gate) // 7:11:13 drift

patchIdeas.name: Seven-step odd meter
patchIdeas.patch: INT mode · Out 6 (÷7) resets a sequencer every seven clocks for 7/8 phrasing
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-160-2:
* Division Mode: INT · Output Mode: Trigger
- Master Clock (Clk) c> A-160-2 (Clock In)
- Master Clock (Clk) c> Sequencer (Clk)
- A-160-2 (Out 6) t> Sequencer (Reset) // 7-step cycle

patchIdeas.name: Reset-locked fills
patchIdeas.patch: Bar-length reset keeps divider phase aligned after tempo changes or on-the-fly re-patching
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-160-2:
* Division Mode: POW2
- Clock (Clk) c> A-160-2 (Clock In)
- Bar Pulse (Trig) t> A-160-2 (Reset In) // re-phase each bar
- A-160-2 (Out 3) c> Fill Gate (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Trigger-mode hi-hat shuffle
patchIdeas.patch: Trigger mode inherits clock pulsewidth — shape clock with narrow gates to get tight hi-hat triggers on divided outputs
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-160-2:
* Division Mode: POW2 · Output Mode: Trigger
- Narrow-PW Clock (Clk) c> A-160-2 (Clock In)
- A-160-2 (Out 1 (÷2)) t> Hat (Trig)
- A-160-2 (Out 2) t> Open Hat (Trig) // short pulses

patchIdeas.name: Slow LFO from divided clock
patchIdeas.patch: POW2 mode · Out 7 (÷128) drives a slew limiter to produce a smooth minute-long pseudo-LFO synced to clock
patchIdeas.diagram:
A-160-2:
* Division Mode: POW2
- Clock (Clk) c> A-160-2 (Clock In)
- A-160-2 (Out 7) >> Slew (In)
- Slew (Out) >> VCF (Cutoff) // slow sweep tied to tempo
